title: drop-epic-label-migration-archive: commit the operational migration script for audit
body:
# Goal

Decide whether to commit the operational migration script from
the 2026-06-28 drop-epic-label work as an audit artifact. The
spec deliberately gitignored it ("one operational script, NOT
committed"), but the final review flagged it as a Minor item
because the planner mutations it caused (64→0 epic:* labels,
9→0 bare epic labels, 20 parent-child edges added, 16 self-
attribution labels stripped) are now only auditable from the
`refs/jjf/*` commit log.

# Background

The script is at `experiments/drop-epic-labels/run.sh` on the
local machine, covered by `experiments/drop-epic-labels/` in
`.gitignore` (line 31). It ran successfully on 2026-06-28 and
pushed the migrated planner state to origin.

A surprise edge case beyond the spec: epics carried
`epic:<their-own-slug>` as a self-label. The script handles
this with a skip-self-dep branch (strip the label, don't add
the cycle-introducing edge). That branch is the most novel
piece of the script and would otherwise be re-derived if
anyone needed to do a similar migration in another jjforge-
hosted project.

# Approach options

- **A. Commit the script + a README.md** documenting "ran
  2026-06-28, mutated N issues, here's the verification grep
  output." Remove the gitignore entry for the directory.
  Future operators in other repos have a working template.
- **B. Leave it gitignored**, on the principle that one-shot
  migrations don't deserve in-tree maintenance burden. The
  planner refs ARE the audit trail; the script is recoverable
  from local backup if anyone needs it again.
- **C. Move just the *novel* logic (the self-attribution skip
  branch) into a doc snippet** in `docs/architecture.md` or
  similar, so the design insight survives even if the script
  itself doesn't.

The reviewer recommended A; the spec called for B. Genuinely
the user's design call.

# Source

Final whole-branch review of the `--parent` feature
(2026-06-28 drop-epic-label) Minor #3.

# Acceptance

Pick one option above and execute it. Spec deviation OK because
the spec is the artifact under review here.
